title: Marcel Hug wins 2026 Boston Marathon men’s wheelchair division, cruises to ninth win
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Marathon The Swiss "Silver Bullet" once again proved unstoppable in Boston.
Marcel Hug won the men's wheelchair division of the Boston Marathon for the ninth time.

Finn Gomez/The Boston Globe By Hayden Bird April 20, 2026 | 10:24 AM 1 minute to read Looking as if he was out for nothing more than a training exercise, an unruffled Marcel Hug emerged alone on Boylston Street to win yet another Boston Marathon in a time of 1:16:06.

title: Bruins’ talk of punishing hockey rings hollow amid ugly Game 1 collapse in Buffalo

title: McCourty twins give their take on what Patriots should target in NFL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots "It’s very hard if it’s just Milton [Williams] and [Christian] Barmore in the middle.
" Devin and Jason McCourty both think the Patriots need to bolster their pass-rushing corps.
AP Photo/Elise Amendola, File The 2026 NFL Draft is just a few days away, and the Patriots still have several holes to fill across their roster at this stage of the offseason.

title: Celtics crowd trolls 76ers fans with ‘We want Boston’ chants during blowout win
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics 76ers fans said they wanted Boston.
Celtics fans mocked them loudly.

Jaylen Brown dunks a basketball at TD Garden Photo by: Barry Chin/Globe Staff During the closing minutes of the 76ers play-in game victory against Orlando that set up this series against the Celtics, fans at Philadelphia’s Xfinity Mobile Arena started a “We want Boston” chant.
